seoTitle: Remove Yahoo Search From Chrome and Stop Hijacking
metaDescription: Yahoo Search taking over your Chrome browser? Follow this guide to remove it, reset your search settings, and stop future browser hijacking attempts.
intro: If Yahoo Search has quietly replaced Google as your default search engine in Chrome, your browser has likely been hijacked by an extension or unwanted software change. This guide covers how to remove it, reset your settings, and prevent it from happening again.

steps.heading: Understand what browser hijacking is
steps.body: Browser hijacking happens when an extension, download, or software bundle changes your search engine or homepage without your consent, often leading to unwanted ads or reduced privacy.

steps.heading: Remove Yahoo Search from Chrome
steps.body: Go into Chrome's search engine settings and switch your default search provider back to Google, removing Yahoo Search from the list of options.

steps.heading: Reset your Chrome browser settings
steps.body: Use Chrome's reset feature to restore your browser's default settings, which clears out lingering changes made by the hijacking software or extension.

steps.heading: Check installed extensions
steps.body: Review your extensions list and remove anything unfamiliar or unnecessary, since hijackers often hide inside browser add-ons.

steps.heading: Prevent future hijacking
steps.body: Be cautious about what you install, avoid bundled software downloads, and periodically check your search settings to catch unwanted changes early.

faq.question: Why did my Chrome search engine change to Yahoo on its own?
faq.answer: This usually happens when a browser extension or bundled software installs itself and silently changes your default search settings.

faq.question: Is removing Yahoo Search from Chrome permanent?
faq.answer: Once you reset your search settings and remove the offending extension, the change should stick unless the same software gets reinstalled.

faq.question: Does resetting Chrome delete my bookmarks?
faq.answer: A standard Chrome settings reset restores default settings like the homepage and search engine but does not delete your saved bookmarks.

faq.question: How can I stop browser hijacking from happening again?
faq.answer: Avoid downloading software from untrusted sources, review extension permissions carefully, and periodically check your search engine settings.
